completed_tasks.name: Cross-boundary smoke test (system Humble Cyclone ↔ pixi Jazzy Cyclone)
completed_tasks.note: Required force-overriding CYCLONEDDS_URI to a multicast-enabled config

completed_tasks.name: Bring up Isaac Sim + control stack + verify topics
Wrist camera ROS2 publisher fix
completed_tasks.note: Built _ReplicatorCameraPublisher (hidden viewport + capture_viewport_to_buffer) — bypasses broken Kit ROS2CameraHelper

completed_tasks.name: RGB encoding (alpha strip)
completed_tasks.note: Done CONSUMER-side in lerobot's _msg_to_ndarray. Doing it in Isaac Sim's render-thread callback throttles RTF to ~1 Hz.

completed_tasks.name: Workspace cam resolution change to 640x480
completed_tasks.note: Resolution is 640x480 but FOV intrinsics are still authored for 16:9 (HFOV=69.4 VFOV=42.5) — 4:3 viewport center-crops the wider FOV → 'zoomed' look. Wrist cam works because it uses square-pixel formula vfov=hfov*H/W.

remaining_tasks.name: Make pick-place actually run during recording
remaining_tasks.note: PRIMARY BLOCKER — robot DID NOT MOVE during 5-episode recording. control_gui's tkinter mainloop is starved under recording load → qs_refresh_all + qs_select services time out → qs_play has no lego selected → arm only moves during initial grasp_home (~3s) then stays still. Mac doesn't hit this because everything runs in one pixi process tree (no DDS cross-boundary contention).

remaining_tasks.name: Fix workspace cam aspect ratio (4:3 → square pixels)
remaining_tasks.note: Change extension.py:1649 — replace `vfov_deg=42.5` with `vfov_deg = hfov_deg * ws_height / ws_width` (= 52.05° for 4:3 at hfov=69.4°). Mirror the wrist cam pattern at extension.py:2342.

remaining_tasks.name: Push reference dataset to HF + run verify_parity.py

blockers.description: Robot does not move during recording — control_gui services time out under DDS+GPU load
blockers.evidence: Driver log shows: init grasp_home succeeds, then qs_refresh_all timeout 15s, qs_select timeout 15s, qs_play 'success=True dispatched' (async ack only), TIMEOUT after 90s. Standalone test (no recording) — same QS cycle completed in 30s.
workaround_options: A. Record AFTER offline trajectory replay — run test_pick_all.sh standalone, log to rosbag, then replay+record in two passes
B. Bypass control_gui entirely — Python driver publishes directly to /arm_controller/joint_trajectory, computes IK from /objects_poses_sim + /drop_poses
C. Reduce concurrent load — drop one camera or lower fps so control_gui mainloop has CPU budget
D. Run the entire stack inside a single pixi env (Mac approach) — but Linux Humble doesn't have robostack equivalent for Python 3.12

blockers.description: Workspace camera 'zoomed' in rerun despite reporting 640x480
blockers.evidence: User: 'workspace camera is zoomed in for some reason though it shows 640x480 wrist cam shows up correctly'. Aspect ratio mismatch: HFOV 69.4° + VFOV 42.5° were authored for 16:9 (1280x720); applying to 4:3 (640x480) center-crops the FOV.
workaround_options: Replace vfov_deg=42.5 with computed vfov_deg = hfov_deg * H/W in extension.py:1649
OR use ws_width=640, ws_height=360 (16:9) instead of 4:3

blockers.description: ROS2 daemon repeatedly enters 'empty node name returned by RMW layer' state under load
blockers.evidence: Multiple times during this session, ros2 CLI commands started failing with xmlrpc Fault. Recovery: ros2 daemon stop && start && wait 3s. Cyclonedds shared memory is fine; issue is daemon's xmlrpc layer getting overwhelmed with too many participants.
workaround_options: Always restart daemon before service call sequences
Use direct rclpy from Python instead of ros2 CLI for all sequences

blockers.description: Multiple ros2 launches accumulate without proper teardown
blockers.evidence: Saw 3 simultaneous control_gui processes at one point (PIDs 2548725, 2575711, 2621619) competing for the /so_arm101_control_gui name. Service calls would route to a random instance.

human_actions_pending.action: Decide which workaround to use for pick-place-during-recording (A/B/C/D in blocker #1)
human_actions_pending.context: Mac approach (D) is structurally cleanest but requires building Linux equivalent. Approach (A) — record-after-replay — is most pragmatic.

human_actions_pending.action: Verify rerun viewer actually shows live data when display is available
human_actions_pending.context: rerun process spawns correctly (port 9876, expect-data-soon) but I never confirmed the GUI window actually appeared — was running headless from Bash subshell. User reported workspace cam zoom in rerun — implying they CAN see it, so display works.

decisions.decision: Use pixi + robostack-jazzy for the lerobot env (not system pip)
decisions.rationale: lerobot pyproject hard-pins Python>=3.12; system Humble is Python 3.10. Jazzy↔Humble IDL works for sensor_msgs/Image and JointState across the version step.

decisions.decision: Force CYCLONEDDS_URI to linux-env/cyclonedds.xml (multicast enabled on `lo`)
decisions.rationale: System default config (~/Documents/ros-mcp-server/cyclonedds_local.xml) has AllowMulticast=false, which blocks cross-process-tree discovery between system Humble and pixi Jazzy.

decisions.decision: Don't rename Isaac Sim publisher topics
decisions.rationale: User direction in this session — keep _sim suffixes. lerobot's BYOH `cameras` config maps any topic name to any feature key, so renaming is unnecessary. Object pose/bbox _sim vs _real are semantically different (USD truth vs ArUco detection) — collapsing them would erase information.

decisions.decision: Strip alpha consumer-side (lerobot _msg_to_ndarray patch), not at Isaac Sim publisher
decisions.rationale: Microbench said numpy alpha-strip was 4.7ms but actual production rate dropped to 1 Hz when stripping at the render-thread callback. Some Kit threshold throttles when callbacks take too long. Consumer-side strip leaves Isaac Sim at 18 Hz.

decisions.decision: Use hidden viewport for wrist cam (_ReplicatorCameraPublisher class), not action graph or replicator annotators
decisions.rationale: Action graph ROS2CameraHelper silently fails to register DDS publisher in Kit 107.3 (confirmed bug, prior session fb450bb8 reached same diagnosis). Replicator annotators + IsaacCamera require rep.orchestrator.step() which is async-only in Kit. Hidden viewport with viewport.updates_enabled=True forced is the only working path.

Sim has no leader/follower split; planner IS the leader. control_gui doesn't publish /joint_commands. drive_base_yaw_sweep.py was a shim. Mirror approximates action ≈ achieved-state for behavioral cloning.

decisions.decision: Workspace camera at 640x480 default, matching wrist
decisions.rationale: User direction. But intrinsics (HFOV/VFOV) authored for 16:9 cause apparent zoom at 4:3 — needs follow-up fix in next session.

uncommitted_files: isaac-sim-mcp:exts/soarm101-dt/so_arm101_dt/extension.py — _ReplicatorCameraPublisher class + workspace cam 640x480 + viewport.resolution force + RunOnce gate restored in setup_wrist_camera_action_graph (forward-compat) + post-graph intrinsic re-authoring

Exploring-VLAs:linux-env/ — NEW directory: pixi.toml, cyclonedds.xml, scripts/{record_sim_isaac.sh, drive_pick_place.py, drive_pick_place_loop.sh, joint_states_to_commands.py, _lib.sh}, symlink to drive_base_yaw_sweep.py
next_action: Read .planning/phases/10.2-lerobot-data-collection-linux-isaac-sim/.continue-here.md FULLY before doing anything. Pick a workaround from blocker #1 (recommend option A or B). Test pick-and-place actually runs during recording BEFORE recording another dataset.
context_notes: 5+ hours of session debugging. Recording infrastructure works perfectly — all topics flow at correct rates (joint_states 50 Hz, wrist 22 Hz, top 22 Hz, joint_commands via mirror 50 Hz). Lerobot writes valid v3 datasets. The remaining blocker is the actual pick-and-place doesn't execute under DDS+GPU load. Standalone (no recording) it works in 30s. Mac doesn't hit this because they run everything in one pixi process tree.
